content: The area of the region(s) enclosed by the curves $y=x^2$ and $y=\sqrt{|x|}$ is
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} \text { Area } & =2 \int_0^1\left(\sqrt{x}-x^2\right) d x=2\left[\frac{2}{3}(x \sqrt{x})-\frac{x^3}{3}\right]_0^1 \\ & =2\left[\frac{2}{3}-\frac{1}{3}\right] \\ & =2 \times \frac{1}{3}=\frac{2}{3} \end{aligned}$$

content: The area of the region containing the points $$(x, y)$$ satisfying $$4 \leq x^2+y^2 \leq 2|x|+|y|$$, is
explanation:
The points in the required region satisfy

$$4 \leq x^2+y^2 \leq 2(|x|+|y|)\quad \text{.... (i)}$$

Since, the curve (i) is symmetrical about both the axes, then the required area is 4 times the area of the region in the 1st quadrant. Therefore, it is sufficient to sketch the region and to find the area in the 1st quadrant.

Area of $$\triangle O A C=1 / 2 \times 4=2$$ units

Area of semi-circle $$=1 / 2 \pi \times 2=\pi$$ unit

The curve has two curves $$x^2+y^2 \geq 4\left(C_1\right)$$ and $$x^2+y^2-2 x-24 \leq 0\left(C_2\right)$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area $$=A \times$$ Area of $$A B C D A$$

$$=4[($$ Area of semi-circle $$A B C A)-($$ Area of segment $$A D C A)]$$

$$=4$$[(Area of semi-circle ABCA) $$-$$ (Area of quadrant OADCO $$-$$ Area of $$\triangle \mathrm{OAC}$$)]

$$=4[\pi-(\pi-2)]=8$$ sq units.

content: The area enclosed by $$y=x^3+1$$ and $$y=x+2$$ in first quadrant, is
explanation:
So,

$$\begin{aligned} A & =\left|\int_\limits0^{1.325} x+2-\left(x^3+1\right) \cdot d x\right| \\ & =\left|\int_\limits0^{1.325}\left(x-x^3+1\right) d x\right|=\left[\frac{x^2}{2}-\frac{x^4}{4}+x\right]_0^{1.325} \\ & =[0.8778-0.7705+1.325] \\ & =1.4323 \approx 1.43 \text { sq units } \end{aligned}$$

Given,

$$y=x^2\quad \text{.... (i)}$$

and $$y=8-x^2\quad \text{.... (ii)}$$

On putting the value of $$y$$ from Eq. (i) to Eq. (ii), we get

$$\begin{aligned} & x^2=8-x^2 \\ & 2 x^2=8 \\ & x^2=4 \\ & x= \pm 2 \end{aligned}$$

$$\therefore$$ The enclosed area $$A B C D A$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\int_{-2}^2\left(8-x^2-x^2\right) d x \\ & =\int_{-2}^2\left(8-2 x^2\right) d x=2 \int_0^2\left(8-2 x^2\right) d x \\ & =2\left[8 x-\frac{2 x^3}{3}\right]_0^2 \\ & =2\left[8(2-0)-\frac{2}{3}\left(2^3-0\right)\right] \\ & =2\left[8 \times 2-\frac{2}{3} \times 8\right] \\ & =2\left[16-\frac{16}{3}\right]=32\left[1-\frac{1}{3}\right] \\ & =32 \times \frac{2}{3}=\frac{64}{3} \text { sq units. } \end{aligned}$$

Given, $$y=x^2$$ and $$y=x+2$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Required area } & =\int_\limits{-1}^2\left((x+2)-x^2\right) d x \\ & =\int_\limits{-1}^2\left(x+2-x^2\right) d x \\ & =\left[\frac{x^2}{2}+2 x-\frac{x^3}{3}\right]_{-1}^2 \\ & =\left(\frac{4}{2}+4-\frac{8}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{1}{2}-2+\frac{1}{3}\right) \\ & =\left(\frac{12+24-16}{6}\right)-\left(\frac{3-12+2}{6}\right) \\ & =\frac{20}{6}+\frac{7}{6} \\ & =\frac{27}{6}=\frac{9}{2} \text { sq units }\end{aligned}$$

content: The area bounded by the circle $$x^2+y^2=16$$ and the line $$y=x$$ in the first quadrant is
explanation:
Given, circle is $$x^2+y^2=16 \ldots$$ (i)

and line is $$y=x\quad \text{.... (ii)}$$

On solving both equations, we get

$$\begin{array}{l} \quad x^2+x^2=16 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 2 x^2=16 \Rightarrow \quad x^2=8 \\ \Rightarrow \quad x= \pm 2 \sqrt{2} \quad \text { and } \quad y= \pm 2 \sqrt{2} \\ \therefore \quad \text { Intersection points are; } \\ P(2 \sqrt{2}, 2 \sqrt{2}) \text { and } Q(-2 \sqrt{2},-2 \sqrt{2}) \end{array}$$

$$\therefore$$ Required area $$=$$ Area of $$\triangle O A P+$$ Area of $$\triangle A P B$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} \times O A \times A P+\int_{2 \sqrt{2}}^4 \sqrt{16-x^2} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \sqrt{2} \times 2 \sqrt{2} +\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{16-x^2}+\frac{16}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)\right]_{2 \sqrt{2}}^4 \end{aligned}$$

$$=4+\left[0+8 \times \frac{\pi}{2}-\sqrt{2} \times 2 \sqrt{2}-8 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =4+\left(4 \pi-4-8 \times \frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ & =4+(4 \pi-4-2 \pi) \\ & =4+2 \pi-4=2 \pi \text { sq units } \end{aligned}$$
